question: How to create a dropdown menu in HTML
description: Use the native <select> element for accessible, form-ready dropdown menus with no JavaScript required.

steps.title: Basic dropdown with <select> and <option>
steps.explanation: The <select> element creates the dropdown container. Each <option> child is a selectable item. The name attribute is used when the form is submitted.
steps.code:
<select name="country">
  <option value="us">United States</option>
  <option value="uk">United Kingdom</option>
  <option value="au">Australia</option>
</select>

steps.title: Add a placeholder prompt
steps.explanation: Use an empty-value <option> with selected and disabled to act as a placeholder. This prevents users from accidentally submitting a blank value.
steps.code:
<select name="country" required>
  <option value="" selected disabled>Choose a country…</option>
  <option value="us">United States</option>
  <option value="uk">United Kingdom</option>
  <option value="au">Australia</option>
</select>

steps.title: Group related options with <optgroup>
steps.explanation: <optgroup label="…"> creates a labelled group of options. The group label itself is not selectable.
steps.code:
<select name="city">
  <option value="" disabled selected>Choose a city…</option>
  <optgroup label="North America">
    <option value="nyc">New York</option>
    <option value="la">Los Angeles</option>
  </optgroup>
  <optgroup label="Europe">
    <option value="lon">London</option>
    <option value="par">Paris</option>
  </optgroup>
</select>

steps.title: Allow multiple selections
steps.explanation: Add multiple to let users select more than one option (Ctrl/Cmd+click). The size attribute sets how many rows are visible without scrolling.
steps.code:
<select name="skills" multiple size="4">
  <option value="html">HTML</option>
  <option value="css">CSS</option>
  <option value="js">JavaScript</option>
  <option value="ts">TypeScript</option>
</select>

full_example.code:
<form action="/submit" method="post">
  <label for="role">Select your role</label>
  <select id="role" name="role" required>
    <option value="" selected disabled>Choose a role…</option>
    <optgroup label="Engineering">
      <option value="frontend">Frontend Developer</option>
      <option value="backend">Backend Developer</option>
      <option value="fullstack">Full-Stack Developer</option>
    </optgroup>
    <optgroup label="Design">
      <option value="ux">UX Designer</option>
      <option value="ui">UI Designer</option>
    </optgroup>
  </select>
  <button type="submit">Submit</button>
</form>
